scenario: Build DevContext: a portable CLI tool for AI-driven working scenarios. Engineers lose 30+ min/day reconstructing context — DevContext bundles repos, skills, knowledge, and session state into resumable packages stored in GitHub. Core features: (1) CLI commands: create, recall, save, list, handoff, teardown, knowledge search. Uses Commander.js. (2) Scenario engine: YAML manifest CRUD, lifecycle management (active/paused/handed-off/archived), JSON Schema validation with ajv. (3) Knowledge wiki: Karpathy-style entity management with Markdown+YAML frontmatter, FTS5 search via better-sqlite3, entity extraction from sessions. (4) Skills system: load/validate Markdown skill files, 5 built-in skills (code-review, ci-monitor, pr-management, session-management, multi-agent). (5) GitHub sync: push/pull scenarios across machines via git, handoff via PR creation. (6) 5 scenario templates (web-api, frontend-app, infra-pipeline, research-paper, multi-agent). Tech: Bun/TypeScript, Commander.js CLI, ajv JSON Schema, better-sqlite3 FTS5, gray-matter, pino, chalk/ora/inquirer. Must have mock providers for all externals. See AGENTS.md and README.md for full architecture. CRITICAL: Zero Microsoft/corporate/internal references — this is public open source.

concerns: Greenfield project with zero existing source code — all ~4000+ lines must be written from scratch across 7 modules, requiring strict coordination on shared types and interfaces
better-sqlite3 native module with Bun runtime may have compatibility issues; need to verify native bindings work before building FTS5 search
GitHub sync module requires careful subprocess git operations (clone, pull, push, branch, PR creation) with proper error handling for network failures and auth edge cases
MCP server protocol implementation must conform to the Model Context Protocol spec for IDE integration — no existing reference code in the project
5 built-in skills and 5 templates are content-heavy deliverables that must be technically accurate and follow the Markdown+YAML frontmatter format consistently
feasibility.recommendation: Proceed with a foundation-first approach: lock down JSON Schemas and TypeScript types before any module work begins. Verify better-sqlite3/Bun compatibility early. Build scenario engine and knowledge wiki in parallel since they share no runtime dependencies. CLI layer comes last since it integrates all modules.

rationale: This is a greenfield TypeScript CLI tool with 7 distinct modules (scenario, knowledge, skills, sync, cli, mcp, providers) that share a common type system and config layer. The team is split by module domain — foundation types, scenario lifecycle, knowledge/search, CLI integration+sync, and content/QA — because each module has distinct technical concerns (ajv validation vs. FTS5 search vs. git operations vs. Commander.js) but all depend on shared schemas and types established by the foundation role.

roles.description: Creates the JSON Schemas (Draft 2020-12), TypeScript type system, shared config module, and pino logger that every other module depends on. Establishes the project's type contracts and validation infrastructure using ajv.
roles.why: Every module (scenario, knowledge, skills, CLI) depends on shared types, schemas, and config. Without a locked-down foundation, parallel development will produce incompatible interfaces. This role removes that bottleneck.

roles.description: Builds the scenario CRUD engine: YAML manifest read/write with js-yaml, lifecycle state machine (active→paused→handed-off→archived), ajv schema validation, and the 5 scenario templates (web-api, frontend-app, infra-pipeline, research-paper, multi-agent).
roles.why: The scenario engine is the core domain — create/recall/save/list/handoff/teardown all operate on scenario manifests. It needs a dedicated role because it combines YAML serialization, JSON Schema validation, lifecycle state transitions, and template instantiation.

roles.description: Builds the Karpathy-style knowledge wiki: entity CRUD using Markdown+YAML frontmatter (gray-matter), FTS5 full-text search via better-sqlite3, entity extraction pipeline, and LLM providers (OpenAI + mock) for automated extraction from sessions.
roles.why: The knowledge system is the most technically complex module — it combines Markdown parsing, SQLite FTS5 indexing, and LLM-powered extraction. These are specialized concerns (native C bindings, search ranking, prompt engineering) that benefit from focused attention.

roles.description: Builds the Commander.js CLI layer with all 7 commands (create, recall, save, list, handoff, teardown, knowledge), the GitHub sync module (git push/pull/clone, PR creation for handoffs), the skills loading/validation/promotion pipeline, and the MCP server for IDE integration.
roles.why: The CLI is the integration layer that wires all modules together, and sync is tightly coupled to CLI commands (save→push, recall→pull, handoff→PR). Skills loading is thin enough to bundle here since it's primarily file I/O with gray-matter parsing. MCP server exposes the same operations as the CLI.

roles.description: Authors all 5 built-in skill Markdown files (code-review, ci-monitor, pr-management, session-management, multi-agent), writes comprehensive unit tests for every module, E2E tests for full scenario lifecycle, example scenarios/entities, and documentation.
roles.why: Content and testing are tightly linked — you can't write meaningful tests without understanding the skill/template/entity formats, and you can't validate content without tests. This role ensures all deliverables work end-to-end and the project ships with real, useful content.

tasks.title: Create JSON Schemas for scenario manifests and knowledge entities
acceptanceCriteria: schemas/scenario.schema.json exists and validates the full scenario manifest format (name, version, status enum, description, repos array, skills array, knowledge array, context object) per AGENTS.md spec
schemas/knowledge-entity.schema.json exists and validates knowledge entity frontmatter (title, type enum, updated date, tags array, related array) per AGENTS.md spec
Both schemas use JSON Schema Draft 2020-12 ($schema: https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema)
ajv can load and compile both schemas without errors (verified by a test script or inline validation)

tasks.title: Create TypeScript types, config module, and pino logger
acceptanceCriteria: src/types.ts exports TypeScript interfaces for Scenario, KnowledgeEntity, Skill, RepoRef, ScenarioContext, and SkillSource matching the JSON Schema definitions
src/config.ts exports a getConfig() function that reads environment variables (DEVCONTEXT_HOME, DEVCONTEXT_LOG_LEVEL, GITHUB_TOKEN) with sensible defaults
src/logger.ts exports a configured pino logger instance with structured JSON output and configurable log level from config
bun run lint (tsc --noEmit) passes with zero errors

tasks.title: Build scenario CRUD with YAML manifest parsing and ajv validation
acceptanceCriteria: src/scenario/manager.ts exports createScenario(), getScenario(), updateScenario(), deleteScenario(), listScenarios() functions
Scenarios are persisted as YAML files (js-yaml) in the DEVCONTEXT_HOME directory and validated against scenario.schema.json via ajv on every read/write
createScenario() generates a UUID, sets status to 'active', timestamps created_at/updated_at, and writes the manifest file
listScenarios() returns all scenario manifests from the storage directory with name, status, and description

tasks.title: Build scenario lifecycle management and status transitions
acceptanceCriteria: src/scenario/lifecycle.ts exports functions: activateScenario(), pauseScenario(), handoffScenario(), archiveScenario() that enforce valid state transitions (active↔paused, active→handed-off, any→archived)
Invalid state transitions (e.g., archived→active) throw a descriptive error
saveScenario() captures current context (summary, open_prs, next_steps, blockers) and updates the manifest with a new timestamp
recallScenario() reads the manifest, validates it, and returns the full scenario state ready for CLI consumption

tasks.title: Create 5 scenario templates with template instantiation logic
acceptanceCriteria: templates/ contains 5 YAML template files: web-api.yaml, frontend-app.yaml, infra-pipeline.yaml, research-paper.yaml, multi-agent.yaml
Each template includes pre-configured repos (placeholder URLs), relevant skills, starter context, and a description matching the template's purpose
src/scenario/templates.ts exports listTemplates() and instantiateTemplate(name, overrides) that creates a new scenario from a template
All 5 templates validate against scenario.schema.json

tasks.title: Build knowledge entity CRUD with Markdown+YAML frontmatter
acceptanceCriteria: src/knowledge/entities.ts exports createEntity(), getEntity(), updateEntity(), deleteEntity(), listEntities() functions
Entities are stored as .md files with YAML frontmatter parsed via gray-matter, in DEVCONTEXT_HOME/knowledge/ directory
createEntity() validates frontmatter against knowledge-entity.schema.json, generates a kebab-case filename from the title
getEntity() returns both parsed frontmatter (as typed object) and raw Markdown body content

tasks.title: Build FTS5 full-text search engine with better-sqlite3
acceptanceCriteria: src/knowledge/search.ts exports a KnowledgeSearch class that creates/opens a SQLite database with an FTS5 virtual table for entity content
indexEntity() extracts title, tags, type, and body text from an entity and upserts into the FTS5 index
search(query) returns ranked results with entity name, title, type, snippet, and relevance score using FTS5 MATCH and rank
rebuildIndex() scans all entity files and re-indexes them, handling additions/deletions/modifications

tasks.title: Build LLM providers for entity extraction (OpenAI + mock)
acceptanceCriteria: src/providers/openai.ts exports an OpenAIProvider class that calls the OpenAI chat completions API to extract knowledge entities from session text
src/providers/mock.ts exports a MockProvider that returns deterministic fake entity extractions without any API calls (for testing)
Both providers implement a shared Provider interface with extractEntities(text): Promise<KnowledgeEntity[]>
src/knowledge/extraction.ts uses the provider interface to extract entities from scenario context/notes and persist them via the entity CRUD

tasks.title: Build skills loading, validation, and promotion pipeline
acceptanceCriteria: src/skills/loader.ts exports loadSkill(name, source) that reads a Markdown skill file, parses YAML frontmatter with gray-matter, and returns a typed Skill object
src/skills/validator.ts validates that skill files have required frontmatter fields (name, description, version, source) and non-empty body content
src/skills/promotion.ts implements the promotion pipeline: personal→team→root with source tracking
loadSkillsForScenario(scenario) loads all skills referenced in a scenario manifest and returns them as an array

tasks.title: Build Commander.js CLI with all 7 commands
acceptanceCriteria: src/cli/index.ts is a valid Bun entry point that creates a Commander program with version, description, and 7 subcommands

Commands implemented: create (interactive with inquirer), recall (by name), save (with --summary flag), list (table output with chalk), handoff (with --to flag), teardown (with confirmation), knowledge (search subcommand)
Each command uses ora for spinners during async operations and chalk for colored output
bun run src/cli/index.ts --help prints usage information with all 7 commands listed

tasks.title: Build GitHub sync module for push/pull and PR-based handoff
acceptanceCriteria: src/sync/git.ts exports pushScenario(), pullScenario(), cloneScenarioRepo() that shell out to git with proper error handling for auth failures and network errors
src/sync/handoff.ts exports createHandoffPR() that creates a GitHub PR (via git push + gh CLI or GitHub API) containing the scenario manifest and context for the recipient
All git operations use the GITHUB_TOKEN from config for authentication
A MockGitProvider exists in src/sync/mock.ts that simulates git operations for testing without network access

tasks.title: Author 5 built-in skill Markdown files with YAML frontmatter
acceptanceCriteria: skills/code-review/skill.md, skills/ci-monitor/skill.md, skills/pr-management/skill.md, skills/session-management/skill.md, skills/multi-agent/skill.md all exist with valid YAML frontmatter (name, description, version, source: root)
Each skill file contains actionable instructions (not placeholder text) with at least 3 sections covering when to use, how to execute, and expected outputs
All 5 skill files parse correctly with gray-matter and contain zero corporate/internal references
skills/ directory contains no other files or directories beyond the 5 defined skill folders

tasks.title: Write comprehensive unit tests for all modules
acceptanceCriteria: tests/unit/ contains test files for scenario (manager, lifecycle, templates), knowledge (entities, search, extraction), skills (loader, validator, promotion), providers (mock), and config
All tests use Bun's built-in test runner (import { describe, test, expect } from 'bun:test') with mock providers for all externals
bun test tests/unit passes with zero failures and covers at least: scenario CRUD, lifecycle transitions, entity CRUD, FTS5 search indexing/querying, skill loading/validation, mock provider extraction
Tests verify error cases: invalid schema validation, invalid state transitions, missing files, malformed YAML/frontmatter

tasks.title: Write E2E tests, example scenarios, example entities, and documentation
acceptanceCriteria: tests/e2e/ contains at least one test that exercises the full scenario lifecycle: create→save→recall→handoff→teardown using the CLI entry point
examples/scenarios/ contains at least 2 example scenario YAML files that validate against the schema
examples/knowledge/ contains at least 2 example knowledge entity Markdown files with valid frontmatter
docs/ contains at least architecture.md (system overview diagram) and getting-started.md (quick start guide matching README)
